message:
chore(beads): start tracking .beads/ configuration alongside issues.jsonl

Flip the repo from "ignore all of .beads/" to "track the beads
configuration and export, ignore only the ephemeral database and
merge artifacts", matching the pattern used in the other FrankenFS-
family repos.

.gitignore:
- Remove the top-level `.beads/` blanket ignore so the beads
  configuration, metadata, and issues.jsonl export are no longer
  silently excluded from the repo.

.beads/.gitignore (new):
- Ignore the ephemeral local pieces that must not land in git:
    * `*.db` / `*.db-journal` / `*.db-shm` / `*.db-wal` — the live
      SQLite store and its WAL/shm companions.
    * `*.lock`, `last-touched`, `*.tmp` — runtime lock and staging.
    * `.br_history/`, `.sync.lock`, `sync_base.jsonl` — per-machine
      sync state and history.
    * `beads.base.jsonl` / `beads.base.meta.json` /
      `beads.left.*` / `beads.right.*` — 3-way merge scratch.
    * `daemon.lock`, `daemon.log`, `daemon.pid`, `bd.sock`,
      `sync-state.json` — beads daemon runtime files.
    * `redirect` — the worktree redirect file.
    * `.bv.lock` — beads-viewer lock file.
  These are the canonical "local only" artifacts the beads runtime
  writes; nothing that ends up in this list represents committed
  project state.

.beads/config.yaml (new):
- Project-level beads configuration captured from the live store:
    * issue_prefix: frankenredis
    * default_priority: 2
    * default_type: task
  Committing this makes the prefix/priority/type conventions a
  versioned part of the repo so new machines and contributors get
  identical behavior without needing to re-run `br init`.

.beads/metadata.json (new):
- Beads store metadata binding the two filenames the runtime treats
  as authoritative: { database: beads.db, jsonl_export: issues.jsonl }.
  Keeping the mapping in-repo prevents drift between the database
  name the runtime expects and the export filename checked into the
  repo.

No other files touched in this commit — the ephemeral
.beads/beads.db.bad_*, .beads/beads.db.pre_rebuild_*, and
.beads/recovery_*/ artifacts that still show as untracked are corruption-recovery backups that should not land in git and are
intentionally left out. Adding patterns for them belongs in a
follow-up tightening of .beads/.gitignore.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,40 @@
+# Database
+*.db
+*.db-journal
+*.db-shm
+*.db-wal
+
+# Lock files
+*.lock
+
+# Temporary
+last-touched
+*.tmp
+
+# Local history backups
+.br_history/
+
+# Sync state (local-only, per-machine)
+.sync.lock
+sync_base.jsonl
+
+# Merge artifacts (temporary files from 3-way merge)
+beads.base.jsonl
+beads.base.meta.json
+beads.left.jsonl
+beads.left.meta.json
+beads.right.jsonl
+beads.right.meta.json
+
+# Daemon runtime files
+daemon.lock
+daemon.log
+daemon.pid
+bd.sock
+sync-state.json
+
+# Worktree redirect file
+redirect
+
+# bv (beads viewer) lock file
+.bv.lock

@@ -0,0 +1,4 @@
+# Beads Project Configuration
+# issue_prefix: frankenredis
+# default_priority: 2
+# default_type: task

@@ -5,7 +5,6 @@
 
 # Local agent/task tooling state
 .bv/
-.beads/
 
 # Local oracle clone (not part of clean-room implementation)
 legacy_redis_code/
